description: There are two classes with the identical fully-qualified name io.paradaux.treasury.utils.Idempotency — one in the plugin (treasury/src/main) and one in the published treasury-api jar (treasury/treasury-api/src/main). The plugin depends on treasury-api, so both land on the classpath with the same package+name (a split-package duplicate). Their sha256 logic is identical, so behaviour happens to agree today, but this is a genuine DRY/structure defect: whichever copy the classloader resolves is non-deterministic, and the two can silently diverge. Contrast with utils/UuidBin.java, which was deliberately turned into a thin alias delegating to :common (ADT-22) instead of a copy — Idempotency should have received the same treatment (delegate to the api copy) rather than being duplicated verbatim.
recommendation: Delete the plugin's io.paradaux.treasury.utils.Idempotency and import the treasury-api io.paradaux.treasury.utils.Idempotency at the 5 call sites (GovServiceImpl, BalanceTaxService, LedgerServiceImpl, SalaryServiceImpl, GovDedupKeys), or make the plugin copy a thin delegating alias exactly as utils/UuidBin does for :common.
verification_note: Verbatim snippet matches treasury/src/main/.../utils/Idempotency.java lines 6-18 exactly. A second class with the identical FQN io.paradaux.treasury.utils.Idempotency exists in treasury-api/src/main, and the plugin declares implementation(project(":treasury:treasury-api")) at build.gradle.kts:33 — so both copies land on the runtime classpath as a split-package duplicate with non-deterministic classloader resolution. Their sha256 logic is behaviourally identical today (same SHA-256/UTF-8 digest) but can silently diverge. The UuidBin contrast is accurate: UuidBin.java was deliberately made a thin delegating alias to :common (ADT-22 documented in-file), the exact treatment Idempotency should have received. Genuine DRY/structure defect; major is defensible because a divergence would corrupt ledger dedup-key derivation.

description: The MiniMessage tag-injection escape sanitize(String) is copy-pasted verbatim in three command classes (GovCommand, FineCommand, TransactionsCommand). It is a security-relevant helper (escapes user-supplied memos/reasons before they reach the MiniMessage renderer), so having three independent copies risks one being updated while the others silently keep the weaker escape. It should be a single shared utility.
recommendation: Extract sanitize() into a shared helper (e.g. a static method on a new commands util or on the Message/i18n layer) and call it from all three command classes so the injection-escape rule lives in exactly one place.

description: The per-transaction-entry rendering loop (formatted+signed+colored amount, memo fallback, sanitize, settlement-time formatting, and the treasury.transactions.entry message send) is duplicated near-verbatim in GovCommand.showHistory and TransactionsCommand.sendEntries. The two even share the same message key and the same em-dash placeholder, so any change to the ledger-history display format must be made in two places to stay consistent.
recommendation: Extract a single shared transaction-entry renderer (a small helper class in commands/ taking Message + AccountService, or a method on a shared base) and have both GovCommand and TransactionsCommand call it.

description: The 'pay from the sender's personal account' orchestration — resolve/create sender account, read balance, pre-check against normalized amount, compose a 'Payment from X to Y[: memo]' memo, transfer, catch IllegalStateException and re-report insufficient funds, then success message — is duplicated three times: PayCommand.payPlayer, PayCommand.payGovernmentAccount, and PayAccountCommand.doPay. Only the recipient resolution differs; the money-movement block is effectively identical. This is business orchestration living (and duplicated) in the command layer that would be better hosted once in a service or a shared pay helper.
recommendation: Extract the shared 'debit sender personal account into target account id' flow (balance pre-check, memo composition, transfer, insufficient-funds handling, success/notify) into one method (a service method or a shared command helper taking senderUuid, targetAccountId, targetLabel, amount, memo) and call it from all three sites.

description: Two sender-related helpers are reimplemented across the command layer. (1) The 'actor UUID from sender' idiom (player UUID, else VIRTUAL_TREASURY_INITIATOR) exists as a private static actorOf in GovCommand, and is inlined verbatim in TreasuryCommand (lines 243-245) and four times in EcoCommand (lines 69, 116, 166, 191). (2) resolvePlayerName(UUID) (Bukkit.getOfflinePlayer -> name-or-uuid.toString) is duplicated identically in GovCommand and FineCommand. Both are cross-cutting command concerns that should be shared once.
recommendation: Add a small shared command-support helper (or static methods) for actorOf(CommandSender) and resolvePlayerName(UUID) and use it from GovCommand, TreasuryCommand, EcoCommand and FineCommand instead of the inline/duplicated copies.

occurrences.snippet: UUID adminUuid = sender instanceof Player p ? p.getUniqueId() : TreasuryConstants.VIRTUAL_TREASURY_INITIATOR;

description: The 'create a primitive GOVERNMENT account (owner=VIRTUAL_TREASURY_OWNER, allowOverdraft=true, creditLimit=-1 sentinel) then seedBalance' block is reimplemented in TaxApiImpl.resolveOrCreateTaxAccount and again in LedgerServiceImpl.getOrCreateGovernmentAccount. Additionally, TaxApiImpl is an api/impl facade that injects AccountMapper directly and does account-writing persistence work, bypassing the AccountService/LedgerService that own government-account creation elsewhere — a layering smell: the same primitive-gov-account invariant (the -1 credit-limit convention) now lives in two write paths that can drift.
recommendation: Route TaxApiImpl's default-tax-account creation through LedgerService.getOrCreateGovernmentAccount (or a shared AccountService method) instead of building the Account and calling AccountMapper inline, so the primitive-GOVERNMENT-account invariant lives in one place and the api/impl layer stops writing accounts directly.
verification_note: Snippet matches TaxApiImpl.resolveOrCreateTaxAccount lines 272-291 exactly. The same primitive-GOVERNMENT-account block (owner VIRTUAL_TREASURY_OWNER, allowOverdraft=true, creditLimit=-1 sentinel, insertAccount + seedBalance) is reimplemented in LedgerServiceImpl.getOrCreateGovernmentAccount (lines 433-444), so the -1 credit-limit invariant lives in two independent write paths that can drift. TaxApiImpl is an api/impl facade injecting AccountMapper directly and performing account-writing persistence, bypassing the LedgerService that owns gov-account creation — a real layering-smell under the project's strict one-directional layering rule. Minor is fair for a DRY/layering issue with no active runtime bug.

description: The business-account name resolution that tries the bare token then falls back to '<token> Corporate Account' (a hardcoded knowledge of FirmServiceImpl's display-name convention) is duplicated in AccountResolver.resolve and GovCommand.doExternalPayout. Encoding another module's naming convention as a magic string in two separate command-layer sites means a firm-naming change silently breaks one path.
recommendation: Centralise business-account-by-name resolution (bare + ' Corporate Account' fallback) in AccountService or reuse AccountResolver from GovCommand.doExternalPayout, so the ' Corporate Account' convention appears once.

description: TreasuryApiImpl injects the concrete TaxApiImpl instead of the TaxApi interface, and its getTaxApi() field is typed TaxApiImpl. Everywhere else in the module DI is done against interfaces (AccountService, LedgerService, MembershipService, DataExportService here; TaxApi is explicitly bound to TaxApiImpl in TreasuryModule line 72). This is an inconsistency with the codebase's inject-the-interface convention and needlessly couples the public API facade to the concrete impl.
recommendation: Change the field and constructor parameter to the TaxApi interface (it is already bound in TreasuryModule) for consistency with the other injected services.
